title: TensorExprKernel::run mutates kernel state under dynamic shapes -> data race on parallel calls
category: data race (shared kernel state mutated by a method documented as safe to call concurrently)
crash_site: TSAN data race / intermittent wrong results when a TorchScript model compiled with the NNC/TensorExpr fuser is executed concurrently (Static Runtime) — the same kernel object is run() from multiple threads.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: prepareRunArgs / runWithAllocatedOutputs (called from run) where the sizes/strides vectors are touched. The defect is run() mutating shared TensorExprKernel state at all when dynamic shapes are present.
ground_truth.fix_summary: TensorExprKernel is created once and run() many times, possibly in parallel. With dynamic shapes, run() modified the kernel's sizes/strides vectors (shared state) -> data race under Static Runtime. Fix: make run() not mutate shared state (compute per-call args without writing them back into the kernel).

admission.note: Authored from PR #73029 body+diff. C1 is MODERATE: the race is in run()'s own mutation of sizes/strides (crash≈cause); the difficulty is realizing run() (documented safe to call repeatedly/in parallel) must be non-mutating, and the write only happens on the dynamic-shapes path. 2022 -> CHECK CONTAMINATION before trusting results. Pure C++ concurrency/state. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/73029 — [NNC] TensorExprKernel state should not be modified on calls to run; fix 7fa092949eec, base 74cd18623ec3.
